regime: Risk-Off Reversal / Strait of Hormuz Shock (Equities and Gold Down, Crude Spikes)
summary: The weekend gap risk we flagged Friday showed up. Over the weekend the US and Iran escalated over the Strait of Hormuz and the tape broke risk-off. Crude was the headline: MCL spiked about 9% to a 78.59 high and flipped long above a 75.79 stop. Equities reversed off Friday's records and tech took the worst of it: MNQ fell 2.12% to close 29,430.75, below a 29,726 stop that flipped short, while MES held up better at -0.95% but still flipped short below 7,594. Gold did not act like a haven, dropping 2.90% to a 3,992.9 low and closing 4,008.7, a deleveraging tell. Both equity trends are now short and volatility expanded. CPI (June) lands Tuesday at 8:30 ET on top of live geopolitical risk, so the book should sit defensive and let the event clear.

keyLevels.label: MNQ ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED SHORT; RTH close 29,431, -295 below. The line that now defines the reversal.

keyLevels.label: MNQ AVWAP / RTH Low
keyLevels.note: Closed below AVWAP; broke the 29,796 OR low and 29,716 O/N low.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop / Record
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED SHORT; rejected the 7,602 record and closed 7,554, -41 below.

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop (MGC1!)
keyLevels.note: Falling SHORT on the front contract; close 4,008.7, tagged a 3,992.9 low. No haven bid.

keyLevels.label: MCL ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: FLIPPED LONG on the Hormuz spike; close 78.01, +2.2 above. Ran to a 78.59 high.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (short side)
setups.entry: Short with the flip on a pullback into the 29,726 (MNQ) / 7,594 (MES) stop, not a chase at the lows
setups.bias: BORDERLINE short. The flip is one session old and CPI is tomorrow; event risk caps size.

setups.name: AVWAP Deviation Band (MNQ, fade only)
setups.entry: Counter-trend fade only on a stretched -3s (29,276) tag with an exhaustion bar and center reclaim
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. Counter-trend into CPI; not a blind fade.

setups.name: MCL (context, not a Net Alpha Pro signal)
setups.entry: No index-stack entry; crude is event-driven and outside the Net Alpha Pro edges
setups.bias: OUT_OF_STACK. Long above 75.79 but headline-sensitive; context only.

event.name: Weekend geopolitical shock (US-Iran, Strait of Hormuz); next scheduled: CPI (June) Tue Jul 14 08:30 ET
event.actual: Reporting indicates the US moved to reimpose a blockade on Iranian vessels using the Strait of Hormuz after renewed US-Iran clashes; tanker traffic through the strait was disrupted.
event.surprise: Supply shock. Crude repriced ~8-9% higher; equities sold the growth and margin hit, gold sold on deleveraging.
event.nqReaction: MNQ fell 2.12% to close 29,430.75, flipping short below a 29,726 stop; tech and semis led lower, so NQ fell roughly twice as hard as ES.
event.crossAsset: MCL +9.09% and flipped long; MES -0.95% flipped short; MGC -2.90% with no haven bid. Asian markets gapped hard (Kospi -8.9%). CPI Tuesday now lands into a stressed tape.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: A large risk-off gap already happened; Tuesday opens into CPI with an unfilled gap toward Friday's 30,069 above. No clean fade with event risk live.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: Short-horizon trend flipped down and the long-horizon base is being tested; alignment is no longer long.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES and MNQ both flipped short below their stops, genuine confirmation of the reversal, not a single-index stop artifact.

risk.throttleState: DEFENSIVE. A geopolitical shock plus tomorrow's CPI is the environment to pull risk in, not add it. Both equity trends flipped short in one session and volatility expanded.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding; the book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact; staying mostly flat protects it into the event.
risk.sizing: Any short is a pullback-into-the-stop entry at reduced size, never a chase at the lows, and it should respect that the flip is one session old. Crude is long above its stop but parabolic and headline-driven, the least sizeable instrument here. Gold is short and extended, so chasing 3,992 lows carries snap-back risk.
risk.note: Week ahead: CPI (June) Tuesday July 14 at 8:30 ET arrives on top of active US-Iran / Strait of Hormuz risk. Per prop rules avoid new positions in the news window and let the initial reaction settle. Trade the level reaction, not the headline.

psychology: Shock days punish two instincts. The first is buying the equity dip because it was just at record highs Friday: that anchor is stale, the stops flipped, and the trend is now down until price proves otherwise. The second is chasing crude after a 9% move because you missed it; a parabolic headline-driven spike is the hardest thing to size and the pullback can be as violent as the pop. Respect the flip: equities short below their stops, crude long above its stop, and none of it is a reason to force the other side. With CPI at 8:30 ET tomorrow and an active geopolitical story, the highest-value discipline today is doing less. Flat is a position.

2MNQ 2-closes back below the 29,814 stopTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTClosed below 29,814 from the globex session on, unwound past the 29,796 OR low and the 29,716 O/N low, printed a 29,386.5 low and closed 29,430.75.
3MES holds the 7,589 stop and the 7,602 record as supportTRIGGERED / FAILEDMES opened RTH at 7,607 above the record and tagged 7,610.5, but could not hold it, rejected and flipped short below the 7,589 stop to close 7,553.75.
4MGC stays below its 4,142 stopTRIGGERED / PLAYED OUTStayed below 4,142 and extended hard, blew through the 4,098 / 4,094 / 4,081 targets to a 3,992.9 low and closed 4,008.7.
5MCL stays below its 73.00 flipped-short stopTRIGGERED / FAILEDThe short voided. MCL 2-closed back above 73.00 and spiked about 9% on the Hormuz shock to a 78.59 high, flipping long.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Graded the Jul 10 map (targeting Jul 13). Row 2 TRIGGERED / PLAYED OUT (MNQ unwound past the 29,796 and 29,716 targets to a 29,386.5 low). Row 4 TRIGGERED / PLAYED OUT (gold stayed below 4,142 and blew past all targets to 3,992.9). Row 3 TRIGGERED / FAILED (MES rejected the 7,602 record and flipped short). Row 5 TRIGGERED / FAILED (the MCL short voided on a ~9% Hormuz spike). Row 1 NOT TRIGGERED (MNQ was already below the stop at the RTH open).
